title: Streams, Super Streams, and Workload Choice · Go Semantics Case: Workload Selection Evidence
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must select queue, stream, or super stream from retention, replay, throughput, partition, client, and operations evidence; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the workload selection evidence increment in a queue-versus-stream Go client decision record. Learners p…
objectives: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Account for stream protocol, replica locality, leader and follower reads, connection routing, confirms, and failure recovery; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Workload Selection Evidence · Theory Workload Selection Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 6752 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to select queue, stream, or super stream from retention, replay, throughput, and partition evidence; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
